daily_brief: The US-Iran war dominated global attention as President Trump escalated threats against Iranian infrastructure, warning that strikes on bridges and power plants 'haven't even started' [BBC, Globe and Mail, Al Jazeera]. French President Macron criticized Trump's contradictory messaging, urging a 'serious' approach and rejecting military action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz [BBC, RFI]. A UN Security Council vote on authorizing force to protect Hormuz shipping was postponed amid Iranian warnings against 'provocative action' [France 24, The Hindu]. The energy crisis deepened as drone attacks struck a Kuwaiti refinery [The Hindu], and European officials warned of 'long-lasting' economic shocks [Financial Times]. In a significant cabinet shake-up, Trump fired Attorney General Pam Bondi, reportedly over frustration with her handling of the Epstein files [BBC, Globe and Mail, Al Jazeera]. Defense Secretary Hegseth also removed US Army Chief of Staff Randy George—an extraordinary wartime firing [Globe and Mail, Le Monde]. Meanwhile, NASA's Artemis II mission successfully executed its translunar injection, sending astronauts toward the Moon for the first time since 1972 [BBC, France 24]. In Southeast Asia, Myanmar's coup leader Min Aung Hlaing was formally elected president by a pro-military parliament [Al Jazeera, The Hindu, ABC Australia].

top_stories.headline: Trump Escalates Iran Strikes, Threatens Power Plants and Bridges as War Enters Second Month
bullets: US bombed the B1 bridge between Tehran and Karaj, killing 8 and wounding 95 according to Iranian state media
Trump posted on social media that the US 'hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran' and threatened electric power plants next
Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi warned the UN Security Council against 'provocative action' ahead of a planned vote on authorizing force to protect Hormuz shipping
A scheduled UN Security Council vote on a resolution to mandate a force protecting Strait of Hormuz shipping was postponed with no new date set

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on Macron's criticism of Trump's inconsistent messaging

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized diplomatic efforts and market impacts alongside military developments

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted civilian infrastructure targeting and included Iranian perspective from former FM Zarif calling for settlement

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Noted 'disturbing trend' of strikes hitting non-military targets

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Reported on regional spillover including Kuwait refinery attack

top_stories.headline: Trump Fires Attorney General Pam Bondi Over Epstein Files Handling
bullets: President Trump removed Pam Bondi as Attorney General, citing a transition to the private sector
Sources indicate Trump was frustrated with Bondi's handling of Jeffrey Epstein file releases and pace of prosecuting his critics
Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, Trump's former personal lawyer, will lead the Justice Department temporarily
Bondi is the second cabinet official removed in recent weeks, following Kristi Noem's departure as Homeland Security chief in March

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Noted Bondi's combative congressional hearings and Trump defending her just hours before firing

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized frustration over prosecution pace of Trump's adversaries

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage focusing on Epstein files connection

top_stories.headline: Macron Criticizes Trump's 'Not Serious' Approach to Iran War, Rejects Military Solution for Hormuz
bullets: French President Macron said handling the Iran war requires seriousness and consistency, stating 'you don't say every day the opposite of what you said the day before'
Macron declared military action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz would be 'unrealistic' as over 40 countries met to discuss the shipping crisis
France and South Korea agreed to cooperate on addressing the energy crisis and securing maritime transport routes through Hormuz
Macron also addressed Trump's comments on reconsidering NATO membership, warning that questioning alliance commitments 'empties it of its substance'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Led with Macron's direct criticism of Trump's communication style

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized France-South Korea defense cooperation and Macron's rejection of military options

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on diplomatic coordination between France and South Korea

top_stories.headline: Myanmar Coup Leader Min Aung Hlaing Formally Elected President by Pro-Military Parliament
bullets: Senior General Min Aung Hlaing received at least 293 of 584 parliamentary votes to become Myanmar's president
The vote formalizes his political control five years after he orchestrated a coup against Aung San Suu Kyi's elected government
The December-January elections that preceded this vote were won by the army-backed Union Solidarity and Development Party with over 80% of seats
Western governments have dismissed the electoral process as a sham designed to legitimize continued military rule

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Detailed the armed resistance that followed the 2021 coup

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Noted Min Aung Hlaing's statement about 'dreams becoming reality'

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Emphasized analyst view that this is a strategic pivot to consolidate power through civilian government veneer

top_stories.headline: NASA's Artemis II Astronauts Complete Translunar Injection, First Humans Beyond Earth Orbit Since 1972
bullets: The Orion spacecraft successfully fired its main engine for 5 minutes and 55 seconds, pushing the crew toward the Moon
Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen became the first non-American to travel to the Moon
The mission spent roughly a day in high Earth orbit testing life-support systems before receiving approval for lunar departure
The crew will travel approximately 250,000 miles to perform a lunar flyby before returning to Earth

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized historical significance and astronaut quotes about humanity's capabilities

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Noted early challenges in orbit and future lunar mission implications

additional_stories.headline: Pentagon fires US Army Chief of Staff Randy George during wartime, an action nearly without precedent
Paris police ban Annual Gathering of Muslims of France citing 'major terrorist risk' from far-right groups
Kuwait oil refinery struck by drone attack, sparking fires at multiple units as regional conflict spreads
Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement, with 12 people sent to the country
Iran war drives up bottled water and beer prices in India as plastic manufacturers struggle with fuel costs
Ancient 2,500-year-old golden Coțofenești helmet recovered more than a year after Dutch museum heist
Cuba pardons over 2,000 prisoners amid US pressure
Australia's fuel supply under pressure as industry warns 53 incoming ships may not all arrive
Suicide car bomb kills five including three women and a child at police station in northwest Pakistan
French National Assembly rejects New Caledonia constitutional reform bill, casting doubt on territory's future
